quality.rationale: Static metric with perfect fluid source requires multi-step derivation combining Killing symmetries, curvature identities, and Einstein equations to prove velocity alignment.

origin.question: Problem 13.9. A static metric is generated by a perfect fluid. Show that the fluid 4-velocity is parallel to the time Killing vector.
origin.solution: Solution 13.9. We use Killing's equation $\xi_{(\alpha ;\beta)} = 0$ and the "hypersurface-orthogonal" statement (see Problem 10.8) of staticity $\xi_{[\alpha ;\beta}\xi_{\gamma ]} = 0$ , to derive$$\begin{array}{l} 0 = (\xi_{[ \alpha ; \beta} \xi_{\gamma ]})^{j \gamma} \\ = \frac {1}{3} \left(\xi_{a; \beta} \xi_{\gamma} + \xi_{\gamma ; a} \xi_{\beta} + \xi_{\beta ; \gamma} \xi_{a}\right)^{i \gamma} \\ = \frac {1}{3} \left(R_{\lambda \gamma \beta a} \xi^{\lambda} \xi^{\gamma} + R_{\lambda \alpha \gamma}^{\gamma} \xi^{\lambda} \xi_{\beta} + R_{\lambda \gamma \beta}^{\gamma} \xi^{\lambda} \xi_{a}\right) \\ \end{array}$$where we have used the result $\xi_{\mu ;a\beta} = \mathbf{R}_{\gamma \beta a\mu}\xi^{\gamma}$ from Problem 10.7. The first term above vanishes by symmetry and the remaining terms give Ricci components, so we have$$\begin{array}{l} 0 = \xi^{\lambda} R_{\lambda [ a} \xi_{\beta ]} = 8 \pi \xi^{\lambda} \left(T_{\lambda [ a} - \frac {1}{2} g_{\lambda [ a} T\right) \xi_{\beta ]} \\ = 8 \pi \xi^{\lambda} \left(\left(\rho + p\right) u_{\lambda} u_{[ \alpha} + \frac {1}{2} (\rho - p) g_{\lambda} [ a)\right) \xi_{\beta ]}. \\ \end{array}$$The last term vanishes trivially so we can conclude $\mathbf{u}_{[\alpha}\xi_{\beta ]} = 0$ and hence $\mathbf{u}\propto \pmb{\xi}$A less rigorous proof is possible if the equivalent “stationary and time reversible” condition for staticity (see Problem 10.8) is used. In this case we see immediately that if $\mathbf{u}$ is not parallel to $\pmb{\xi}$ then $T^{0i} \neq 0$ in the “time reversible frame.” But this implies $G^{0i} \neq 0$ which is incompatible with time reversibility of the metric.
